Fact.Description: Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income

Fact.Name: EITC QC Of Another
Fact.Description: Whether the TP or their spouse, if applicable, could be someone else's QC for EITC

Fact.Name: EITC QC Of Another Required to file
Fact.Description: Whether the person who can claim TP or SP as a QC for EITC is required tofile

Fact.Name: EITC QC Of Another is filing
Fact.Description: Whether the person who can claim TP or SP as a QC for EITC is filing

Fact.Name: EITC QC Of Another is filing
Fact.Description: Whether the person who can claim TP or SP as a QC for EITC is filing for a refund only

Fact.Name: Fails EITC age band without qualifying children (age range buckets)
Fact.Description: True when no one in scope is in the "Between 25 and 64" age band at the end of the tax year, for purposes of the EITC without a qualifying child. Uses /ageRange and /ageRangeSecondary only (not DOB). When MFJ and the flow collects spouse age (/flowShouldSeeSpouseAgeRange), either filer in that band passes; otherwise only the primary filer's /ageRange is used.

Fact.Name: User declines EITC qualifying children (for disqualifier)
Fact.Description: True when the filing-status question is complete and the taxpayer answered that they do not plan to claim EITC qualifying children. For-disqualifier only: does not read /eitcQualifyingChildren, which can stay incomplete when /lesserOf operands are incomplete.

Fact.Name: Filing status allows EITC without qualifying children (for disqualifier)
Fact.Description: Mirrors the MFJ-or-not-married intent of /maybeEligibleForEitcWithoutQc without relying on /isMarried when /maritalStatus was never collected (knows-filing-status path). Uses /isFilingStatusSingle when /filingStatus is complete. For-disqualifier only; does not change /isMarried or /maybeEligibleForEitcWithoutQc.

Fact.Name: Not blocked as another's dependent for EITC (for disqualifier)
Fact.Description: True when /treatFilersAsDependents is known false, or /isClaimedAsDependent is known false, or derived filing status is single with filing complete. For-disqualifier bridge when /treatFilersAsDependents stays incomplete because /primaryFilerIsClaimedOnAnotherReturn is not collected. Does not redefine /treatFilersAsDependents.

Fact.Name: Disqualified for EITC due to age without qualifying children
Fact.Description: True when the taxpayer does not qualify for EITC without a qualifying child because they (or their spouse on a joint return, when that age is collected) are not in the "Between 25 and 64" age band at the end of the tax year. Uses /ageRange (and /ageRangeSecondary when applicable), not DOB. Uses ForDisqualifier bridge facts so /eitcQualifyingChildren, /isMarried, and /treatFilersAsDependents need not be changed globally.

Fact.Name: Disqualified for EITC due to age with qualifying children
Fact.Description: True when the taxpayer has QCs and they should see the age comparison Test but there are no tentatively eligible QCs for EITC. Used in Testing eligibility

Fact.Name: Disqualified for EITC due to age without qualifying children
Fact.Description: True when the taxpayer does not qualify for EITC without a qualifying child based on the age test, and when the filer is NOT disqualified due to non-MFJ filing status disqualifications. Used to ensure that only the correct knockout is shown to the taxpayer.

Fact.Name: Results guard: QC entry cohort but zero EITC qualifying children
Fact.Description: True when /flowCohortEitcRequiresQualifyingChildEntry applies (user must enter at least one EITC QC story), /familyAndHousehold is complete, and /eitcQualifyingChildren equals 0. Used so /maybeEligibleForEitc cannot read as eligible when the household list is resolved but no EITC QCs remain. Gating on a complete /familyAndHousehold avoids treating an unset collection as “zero children” mid-flow.

Used for a tentative eligibility check for the flow. Until the taxpayer is able to enter their number of qualifying dependents, assume they may be eligible based on the greatest phaseout amount for their filing status.
Whether the TP's AGI is low enough to potentially be eligible for the EITC

Fact.Name: Show AGI knockout on Adjustments
Fact.Description: True when tentative AGI is over the highest EITC phaseout line and the user has clicked Continue from Adjustments once (see /flowClickedNextOnAdjustmentsPage), so the knockout does not appear before that interaction.
Whether the TP's Earned Income is low enough to potentially be eligible for the EITC.

Fact.Name: EITC disqualified: AGI or earned income at or above completed phase-out line
Fact.Description: True when /belowEitcAgiLimit or /belowEitcEarnedIncomeLimit is false (Pub 596: must be strictly less than the applicable limit from filing status and number of EITC qualifying children). Used after QC count is known.

Fact.Name: Qualifying Children page: prerequisites to evaluate final EITC income limits
Fact.Description: True when the taxpayer has finished claiming vs not claiming qualifying children and, if claiming, the household collection is complete so /eitcQualifyingChildren reflects the entered stories.

Fact.Name: Show EITC income-limit knockout on Qualifying Children after Continue
Fact.Description: True when prerequisites are met and AGI or earned income is at or above the applicable completed phase-out amount. The browser sets /flowClickedNextOnQualifyingChildrenPageForIncomeLimit on first Continue to reveal the alert (mirrors Adjustments AGI knockout).

Fact.Name: Show EITC income-limit knockout after Continue on Qualifying Children
Fact.Description: Wraps /flowShouldRevealEitcIncomeLimitOnQualifyingChildrenContinue so the knockout appears only after the user has clicked Continue once (see /flowClickedNextOnQualifyingChildrenPageForIncomeLimit).
